title: Implement Tactus host Python module registration API
description:
# Implement Tactus host Python module registration API

## What

Add the Tier 1 host-module API in the Tactus repo so Plexus can later do:

```python
runtime.register_python_module("plexus", plexus_module)
```

and Lua can do:

```lua
local plexus = require("plexus")
```

## Scope

- Inspect current Tactus `LuaSandbox`, `StdlibModuleLoader`, and `TactusRuntime` APIs.
- Add a public runtime API for registering Python-backed host modules.
- Extend the safe require loader to resolve host-registered names while preserving existing `tactus.*` stdlib loading.
- Add tests for registered module import, unknown module rejection, and stdlib compatibility.
- Run focused Tactus tests only.

## Constraints

- Do not add Plexus itself to Tactus stdlib.
- Do not open arbitrary filesystem/module loading.
- Preserve sandbox restrictions (`package.cpath` empty, controlled Python attrs, no native code loading).
- No commits without explicit approval.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text:
Progress: implemented the Tier 1 host Python module registration API in the Tactus repo on branch `feature/host-python-modules`.

Tactus changes made:
- `tactus/core/lua_sandbox.py`
  - Added optional `python_modules` argument to `LuaSandbox`.
  - Added `LuaSandbox.register_python_module(name, module)`.
  - Passed host modules into the existing safe Python loader.
  - Reserved `tactus.*` namespace for stdlib only.
- `tactus/stdlib/loader.py`
  - Extended loader to resolve explicit host-registered modules before falling back to `tactus.*` stdlib modules.
  - Added `register_host_module` and host object-to-Lua table conversion.
  - Supports nested host module namespace objects and public callable methods while skipping private attrs / modules / classes.
- `tactus/core/runtime.py`
  - Added `TactusRuntime.register_python_module(name, module)` public host API.
  - Runtime passes registered modules into each new LuaSandbox during execute.

Tests added/updated:
- `tests/stdlib/test_require_python.py`
  - registered host module can be required by name and nested namespace method called
  - module can be registered after sandbox creation
  - unknown host module remains unavailable
  - reserved `tactus.*` namespace cannot be used for host modules
  - runtime-level `register_python_module` works during `execute(format="lua")`

Focused verification run in `/Users/ryan/projects/Tactus`:
```bash
python -m pytest tests/stdlib/test_require_python.py -q
```
Result: `15 passed`.

No commits made. Task remains `in_progress` until reviewed/landed.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text:
Follow-up hardening: changed Tactus loader precedence so explicit host modules cannot be shadowed by local `.tac` files.

Reason: if Plexus registers `plexus`, `require("plexus")` must reliably return the host capability module. A local `plexus.tac` in the procedure base path should not be able to intercept that import.

Implementation adjustment:
- Split host and stdlib Python searchers.
- Host searcher is inserted before filesystem searchers (after package.preload).
- Stdlib Python searcher remains appended after `.tac` searchers, preserving existing Tactus-first behavior for `tactus.*` stdlib imports.

Added test:
- `test_host_module_preferred_over_local_tac_file`

Focused verification in `/Users/ryan/projects/Tactus`:
```bash
python -m pytest tests/stdlib/test_require_python.py -q
```
Result: `16 passed`.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text:
Follow-up hardening completed for host module registration.

Additional changes:
- Added shared host module name validation: names must be dotted identifiers like `plexus` or `vendor.module`.
- Constructor-supplied `python_modules={...}` now uses the same validation as `register_python_module`.
- Re-registering a module now clears Lua `package.loaded[name]`, so subsequent `require(name)` calls see the updated host module instead of a stale cached Lua table.

Additional tests:
- re-registering a host module updates subsequent `require()` calls
- constructor-supplied `tactus.*` host module is rejected
- empty/path-like/hyphenated/number-leading host module names are rejected

Verification in `/Users/ryan/projects/Tactus`:
```bash
python -m pytest tests/stdlib/test_require_python.py -q
python -m py_compile tactus/core/lua_sandbox.py tactus/core/runtime.py tactus/stdlib/loader.py tests/stdlib/test_require_python.py
```
Result: `19 passed`; compile clean.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text:
Added Tactus developer documentation for host-registered Python modules.

Changes in `/Users/ryan/projects/Tactus`:
- Updated `docs/SANDBOXING.md` with a Host-Registered Python Modules section.
- Documented `runtime.register_python_module("plexus", plexus_module)` usage and Lua `require("plexus")` import.
- Documented security properties:
  - host modules are explicit per-runtime capabilities
  - names must be dotted identifiers
  - `tactus.*` remains reserved for stdlib
  - host modules resolve before local `.tac` files
  - stdlib Python modules remain fallback after `.tac` searchers
  - re-registration clears Lua require cache
- Updated `tactus/core/lua_sandbox.py` module docstring so it no longer says `require()` only loads `.tac` files.

Verification in `/Users/ryan/projects/Tactus`:
```bash
python -m pytest tests/stdlib/test_require_python.py -q
python -m py_compile tactus/core/lua_sandbox.py tactus/core/runtime.py tactus/stdlib/loader.py tests/stdlib/test_require_python.py
```
Result: `19 passed`; compile clean.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text:
Hardened host module object introspection in Tactus.

Problem:
- The loader previously used `dir(obj)` + `getattr(obj, name)` for ordinary host objects.
- That can evaluate `@property` attributes while constructing the Lua module table.

Change:
- Added `_host_module_items()` in `tactus/stdlib/loader.py`.
- For ordinary objects, it now collects public instance attributes from `__dict__` and public class callables via `inspect.getmembers_static(type(obj))`.
- Properties are skipped rather than evaluated.
- Class methods are still bound through `getattr()` only after static inspection confirms they are callable.

Test added:
- `test_host_module_registration_does_not_evaluate_properties`

Verification in `/Users/ryan/projects/Tactus`:
```bash
python -m pytest tests/stdlib/test_require_python.py -q
python -m py_compile tactus/core/lua_sandbox.py tactus/core/runtime.py tactus/stdlib/loader.py tests/stdlib/test_require_python.py
```
Result: `20 passed`; compile clean.
